tldr: Graduate from Vatstack's lightweight VAT validation/rates API to Fonoa's broader tax determination, invoice validation, and e-invoicing stack.
why_switch: Outgrowing simple VAT number validation and rate lookups; need full determination
Want e-invoicing/compliance coverage for multiple countries, not just data lookups
Need stronger enterprise support and roadmap than a smaller vendor can offer
Consolidating tax tooling onto a single provider for global SaaS
Looking for real-time invoice validation alongside VAT data
what_to_export: API usage logs and cached VAT validation results (for audit)
Customer VAT numbers and validation timestamps
Country-by-country VAT rate tables you currently rely on
Webhook/event history if you persist Vatstack callbacks
Mapping of Vatstack response fields to your internal schema
Free vs paid plan limits you've been hitting (sizing input)
Any custom rules built on top of Vatstack outputs
List of integrations consuming the Vatstack API key
export_method: Vatstack is primarily a stateless validation/rate API, so most of the data lives in your own systems rather than in Vatstack. Pull customer VAT IDs and historical validation outcomes from your own database, and use the Vatstack dashboard (per their official docs) for any account-level reports or cached records you need. There is generally no large dataset to migrate — focus on identifying every caller of the Vatstack endpoint.
import_method: Provision a Fonoa tenant following their official onboarding documentation, configure your tax registrations and customer data, and replace Vatstack API calls with the equivalent Fonoa endpoints for VAT validation, tax determination, and (where applicable) e-invoicing. Run both APIs in parallel for a sample of traffic to confirm parity before flipping a feature flag, and revoke the Vatstack key once nothing depends on it.

concept_mapping.note: Confirm exact endpoint name and response shape in Fonoa docs
Determination usually returns more than just a rate — adapt callers
Different billing model; size against current call volume
Re-test edge cases you patched around Vatstack
Validate current pricing on Fonoa's official pricing page; their model is enterprise-led, expect a quote rather than a public rate card
Check if Fonoa offers an SDK in your language

gotchas: Fonoa is a much larger surface area than Vatstack — resist re-implementing only what Vatstack did
Response shapes will differ; do not assume drop-in compatibility
Rate-limit and pricing characteristics differ — model your call volume and confirm pricing in current docs before committing
Some Vatstack edge cases (e.g. unusual country results) may behave differently — re-test
Keep historical Vatstack validation receipts for audit trails

email.subject: Vatstack -> Fonoa migration plan
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposing we move VAT tooling from Vatstack to Fonoa. Vatstack has been solid for validation and rates, but we need full tax determination and e-invoicing as we scale across more markets.

Plan:
1. Inventory every Vatstack caller in our codebase.
2. Stand up a Fonoa tenant per their official docs.
3. Wrap our existing tax helper so we can run both APIs in parallel behind a flag.
4. Compare results on real traffic, then cut over and revoke the Vatstack key.

Estimated effort 1-3 weeks. Main risks are response-shape differences and pricing model change (Fonoa is enterprise-led; we will need a quote). Please review.

Thanks
